strands.title: hamutay Phase 1 EXECUTION — CYCLE 48 VERIFICATION & REAL DATA TESTING
strands.content: CYCLE 48 INTEGRATION: Phase 1 execution begins with CONCRETE DATA VALIDATION. CYCLE 47 planning specified verification + test data acquisition. CYCLE 48 ACTIONS COMPLETED: (1) run_projection.py FILE CREATED SUCCESSFULLY (turn 142, tool_result confirms /home/tony/projects/hamutay/src/hamutay/run_projection.py written to disk). (2) REAL PICHAY SESSION LOG ACCESSED (turn 144, user initiated file read on actual Pichay baseline_run1 session JSONL). This is CRITICAL: hamutay pipeline is moving from abstract design to EXECUTION on real conversation data. PHASE 1 CHECKPOINT — READINESS: run_projection.py is now RESIDENT in filesystem, importable as hamutay.run_projection module. Real Pichay session logs are ACCESSIBLE at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/. CYCLE 48 NEXT: need to EXAMINE session JSONL structure (what fields, how many turns, content format) to confirm log_reader.read_session_jsonl() will parse correctly. Then EXECUTE run_projection.py on this real data. Current status: verification phase ~50% complete (file exists, data accessible). Actual execution (calling run_projection.py CLI) still pending. EXPECTATION: session JSONL contains ConversationTurn entries (turn_number, role, content) that log_reader can parse. If malformed or missing fields, will surface now during Cycle 48 data inspection.

key_claims.text: run_projection.py file created successfully (turn 142): /home/tony/projects/hamutay/src/hamutay/run_projection.py exists on disk, ready for import.
Real Pichay session JSONL located and accessible (turn 144): /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl readable.
Phase 1 execution transitioning from abstract design to concrete data: moving from 'is the design sound?' to 'does it work on real logs?'
Cycle 48 checkpoint: verification phase ~50% complete (file + data access confirmed). Execution still pending (requires CLI invocation + Haiku API calls).

strands.title: Data Validation & log_reader.read_session_jsonl() Compatibility (CYCLE 48)
strands.content: CYCLE 48 CRITICAL: session JSONL inspection in progress (turn 144, file read initiated). REQUIRED VALIDATION CHECKS: (1) STRUCTURE: Does JSONL file contain JSON records per line (one turn per line)? Or is it a single JSON array? (2) FIELD PRESENCE: Does each record have turn_number, role, content fields that log_reader.read_session_jsonl() expects? (3) CONTENT QUALITY: Are 'content' fields non-empty strings? Do they contain actual conversation text (user queries, assistant responses)? (4) TURN COUNT: How many turns in session? (~5-10 = quick test, ~50+ = realistic conversation, >100 = long conversation stress test). CYCLE 48 PENDING: turn 144 file read (limit 3) will show first 3 lines of JSONL. Must inspect to confirm structure matches log_reader expectations. POTENTIAL ISSUES: (a) If session JSONL has 'messages' array (nested structure per Pichay Phase 4a design), log_reader.read_session_jsonl() may fail (expects flat ConversationTurn, not messages object). Workaround: either extend log_reader to handle messages field, or manually extract turn_number + content from messages. (b) If JSONL is actual JSON array (not line-delimited), read_session_jsonl() will fail on first line parse. Fixable with wrapper. (c) If content field is missing/empty, turns are invalid for projection. DECISION POINT (after file inspection): IF session structure is COMPATIBLE with log_reader → proceed to run_projection.py execution. IF INCOMPATIBLE → quick log_reader patch in Cycle 48 required before execution.

key_claims.text: log_reader.read_session_jsonl() expects: JSONL format (one JSON per line), ConversationTurn structure (turn_number, role, content fields).
Potential incompatibility risk: if Pichay session JSONL has 'messages' nested field (Pichay structured format), log_reader may not parse correctly.
Cycle 48 validation: turn 144 file inspection will reveal actual session structure. Compatibility assessment determines whether run_projection.py can execute immediately or requires log_reader patch.

strands.title: Phase 1 EXECUTION PATHWAY — REVISED (CYCLE 48 CONTEXT)
strands.content: CYCLE 48 REVISION: Cycle 47 instructions_for_next specified Phase 1 verification + test data acquisition. CYCLE 48 COMPLETION STATUS: (1) ✓ File created (run_projection.py exists), (2) ✓ Data accessed (real Pichay session JSONL located), (3) PENDING: structure validation (file inspection in progress, turn 144), (4) PENDING: execution (CLI invocation + Haiku API). REVISED EXECUTION SEQUENCE (Cycles 48-49): CYCLE 48 TASKS: (a) Complete data validation: inspect session JSONL structure (turn 144 output analysis), confirm log_reader compatibility, (b) IF compatible: execute run_projection.py on session (python -m hamutay.run_projection ...), collect tensor trajectory + terminal stats, ~20-45 minutes + Haiku API time, (c) IF incompatible: patch log_reader in Cycle 48, then execute in Cycle 49. CYCLE 49 TASKS (if Cycle 48 execution successful): (a) Analyze trajectory output: strand stability, compression ratio, declared losses, (b) Decision: SANE or PATHOLOGICAL? (c) If SANE: proceed to Riemann metrics (Phase 1+ parallel), (d) If PATHOLOGICAL: diagnose root cause (Cycle 49-50 debugging). COMPRESSED TIMELINE: Target = Phase 1 trajectory analysis complete by end Cycle 49. Riemann metrics (Phase 1+) begin Cycle 49-50 if sane. RISK: If session JSONL has complex structure (messages field, nested content), log_reader patch may consume all of Cycle 48, pushing execution to Cycle 49. Acceptable — Phase 1 still on track.

key_claims.text: Cycle 48 checkpoint status: file creation ✓, data access ✓, validation PENDING, execution PENDING.
Revised execution sequence: Cycle 48 validation + possible execution, Cycle 49 analysis + decision (SANE/PATHOLOGICAL). Target = Phase 1 findings by end Cycle 49.
Risk mitigation: if log_reader patch required, Cycle 48 absorbed by debugging, execution shifts to Cycle 49. Phase 1 timeline still viable.

strands.title: pichay Phase 4a Status — STILL DEFERRED BUT POTENTIALLY ACTIVATED BY CYCLE 48 DATA INSPECTION
strands.content: CYCLE 48 OBSERVATION: Data validation (turn 144) inspecting actual Pichay session JSONL structure. If session contains 'messages' field (complex nested structure per Pichay Phase 4a design), this reveals: (1) Phase 4a structured message format is LIVE in baseline_run1 data, (2) log_reader.read_session_jsonl() must be EXTENDED to handle messages field OR simple wrapper created to flatten ConversationTurn from messages array. CYCLE 48 CONDITIONAL: IF session JSONL shows messages field: → Phase 4a ACTIVATION REQUIRED (not optional). Must implement messages parsing to unblock Phase 1. IF session JSONL shows flat turn_number/role/content: → Phase 4a remains DEFERRED. log_reader works as-is. CURRENT STATUS: pichay Phase 4a formally deferred from Cycle 47, but Cycle 48 data inspection may force re-prioritization. DECISION AUTHORITY: File inspection (turn 144) determines whether Phase 4a is blocking (activate) or non-blocking (defer). Will know within 10 minutes of processing file read output.

key_claims.text: pichay Phase 4a remains formally deferred from Cycle 47, but Cycle 48 data inspection may force activation if session JSONL has messages field.
If Pichay baseline_run1 session has structured messages format: Phase 4a becomes REQUIRED to unblock Phase 1 (not optional parallel work).
Decision point will emerge from turn 144 file inspection: Phase 4a activation decision made immediately after structure assessment.

strands.title: Cycle 48 Open Questions & Unresolved Technical Details
strands.content: CYCLE 48 INTRODUCES NEW OPEN QUESTIONS from data validation: (1) SESSION STRUCTURE: Does baseline_run1 session JSONL contain 'messages' field (Pichay Phase 4a structure) or flat turn/role/content? Determines Phase 4a activation. (2) TURN CONTENT FORMAT: Are 'content' fields raw text strings, or do they contain structured metadata (tokens, latency, model info)? log_reader expects raw text. (3) BASELINE_RUN1 COMPLETENESS: How many turns in d5da3c7a... session? Is it a complete conversation, or partial/failed run? (4) HAIKU API RATE LIMITS: Running run_projection.py on 50+ turns will generate 50+ Haiku API calls. Will this hit rate limits? Should we implement call batching or delays? (5) PRIOR TENSOR INITIALIZATION: In run_projection.py, first call to projector.project() has no prior_tensor (is None). Does projector handle initialization gracefully? (turn 144 file inspection will inform subsequent API call testing). CARRIES FORWARD FROM CYCLE 47: (6) Does run_projection.py CLI interface actually work (argparse integration, --output_dir, --max_turns)? Not tested yet. (7) Does print_tensor_trajectory() correctly compute strand title sets across cycles? (8) What are concrete SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL thresholds (e.g., >50% strand churn = pathological)? (9) Is hamutay package __init__.py properly configured for imports? CYCLE 48 PRIORITY: Questions 1-5 (data validation + execution readiness) must be answered before run_projection.py execution.

key_claims.text: Cycle 48 critical open question: does baseline_run1 session JSONL have 'messages' field (activates Phase 4a) or flat structure (defers Phase 4a)?
Data validation (turn 144) will surface structural incompatibilities with log_reader. If issues found, Cycle 48 log_reader patch required before execution.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed analysis of prior Cycle 47 strand content (e.g., specific token estimates, exact compression formulas). Cycle 48 focuses on EXECUTION not theory refinement.
declared_losses.why: Context pressure: Cycle 48 content is data validation + execution readiness, not theoretical deepening. Prior strands consolidated into execution pathway.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Explicit discussion of Riemann metric integration (riemann.py design). Deferred to Phase 1+ pending Phase 1 execution outcome (sane vs pathological decision).
declared_losses.why: Practical constraint: riemann.py development blocked on run_projection.py execution + trajectory analysis. Cannot build Riemann integration until Phase 1 data available.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Detailed CLI argument specifications for run_projection.py (argparse integration, help text, parameter validation). File created, but CLI interface NOT YET TESTED.
declared_losses.why: Authorial choice: Cycle 47 designed CLI interface conceptually. Cycle 48 focuses on data validation + execution. CLI testing deferred to execution phase.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Extended Phase 1 pathway details from Cycle 47 (multiple conversation testing, statistical aggregation). Cycle 48 narrows focus to SINGLE conversation execution first.
declared_losses.why: Practical constraint: must validate single conversation works before scaling to multiple. Cycle 48-49 focused on minimal viable execution.

open_questions: What is the actual structure of baseline_run1 session JSONL? Does it have 'messages' nested field (Pichay Phase 4a format) or flat turn_number/role/content? [CRITICAL for Cycle 48 — turn 144 file inspection should answer this]
If session JSONL has messages field, does log_reader.read_session_jsonl() need patching to extract ConversationTurn? Can it be done in <30 minutes, or requires Phase 4a full implementation?
How many turns are in the baseline_run1 session? ~5-10 (quick), ~50 (realistic), or >100 (stress test)? Impacts run_projection.py execution time estimate.
Does projector.project() handle prior_tensor=None on first cycle? Does it correctly initialize tensor, or does it require explicit prior tensor?
Will run_projection.py Haiku API calls hit rate limits on 50+ turns? Should we implement call delays or batching?

Has hamutay.__init__.py been configured to export Tensor, Projector, log_reader, run_projection? Can it be imported as 'from hamutay import run_projection' or only 'from hamutay.run_projection import run_projection'?
What does Tensor.token_estimate() method actually compute? Is it word count, JSON serialization size, or Haiku token count estimate?
After run_projection.py execution produces trajectory, what are concrete SANE vs PATHOLOGICAL decision thresholds? (e.g., >50% strand churn per cycle = pathological?)
Should Phase 1 execution be SINGLE conversation (one run_projection.py invocation) or MULTIPLE conversations? Cycle 48 focuses on single; Cycle 49+ addresses scaling.
If Phase 4a activation is required (messages field present), should log_reader patch or full Phase 4a implementation be prioritized? Time/scope trade-off?
